title: Join ENCATC’s Online Session on Increasing Research Visibility
content: Researchers and professionals working in cultural heritage, cultural management and related disciplines are invited to join an upcoming online session organised by ENCATC's Research Platform, in collaboration with the European Journal of Cultural Management and Policy and Frontiers.

title: EIT Culture & Creativity launches EUR 600 000 in funding for skills-based training in the Audio-Visual Media sector
content: The Audio-Visual Media (AVM) sector is undergoing rapid technological, market, and regulatory change, creating urgent new skills needs across areas such as artificial intelligence, intellectual property, and digital storytelling.  EIT Culture & Creativity has launched an open call for proposals supporting the development and delivery of skills-based Continuous Professional Development (CPD) courses for Europe’s AVM […]

title: A Day of Ideas, Innovation, and Collaboration
content: On 29 May 2026, the Time Machine Organisation community came together online for the TMO Community Showcase: 3D Acquisition, Reconstruction and Applications — a day dedicated to exploring how 3D technologies are transforming the way we document, preserve, and experience cultural heritage. What made the event particularly special was not only the impressive variety of […]

title: Highlights Twin it! Part II Discussions and European Collaboration
content: Two initiatives closely connected to the Time Machine Organisation community — 3D-4CH – Online Competence Centre in 3D for Cultural Heritage and 3DBigDataSpace — contributed to these important discussions.

title: Strengthening Europe’s 3D Cultural Heritage Ecosystem
content: Recently, TMO joined partners and stakeholders in Poznań for a series of events highlighting the progress and impact of two important European initiatives: 3DBigDataSpace and 3D-4CH – Online Competence Centre in 3D for Cultural Heritage.

title: CIPA Heritage Documentation Summer School 2026 in Batumi, Georgia
content: The Summer School offers an immersive, hands-on learning experience focused on architectural and cultural heritage documentation.
snippet: The Summer School offers an immersive, hands-on learning experience focused on architectural and cultural heritage documentation.

title: INFINITY Project Researchers Recognised with Best Paper Awards at ESWC / SemDH 2026
content: The INFINITY Project have been recognised with two Best Paper Awards at ESWC / SemDH 2026.

title: Contribute to “Robotics and Physical Artificial Intelligence for Cultural Heritage”
content: The journal npj Heritage Science has launched a dedicated collection entitled “Robotics and Physical Artificial Intelligence for Cultural Heritage”, inviting submissions of original research articles that advance both cultural heritage and robotics-related fields.

title: New Self-Assessment Tool Helps Archives Improve Sustainability and Reduce Costs
content: A new online self-assessment tool developed through the EU Sustainable Archives and Greener Approaches (SAGA) project offers archival institutions a practical way to evaluate their environmental performance and identify opportunities for improvement.

title: Open Maps Explores New Research Directions for Digitised Historical Cartographic Material
content: A new working paper from the Open Maps initiative explores these questions and presents emerging research directions and methodological approaches for working with digitised historical cartographic material.

title: How Cultural Heritage Can Lead the Way
content: A new publication from the common European data space of cultural heritage explores how Public AI can be developed and strengthened through the rich, diverse, and trusted resources of cultural heritage institutions across Europe.

title: Call for Papers Now Open: DTI Conference Prague 2026
content: Taking place from 18–20 November 2026 at the historic Břevnov Monastery in Prague, the conference will bring together archivists, researchers, cultural heritage professionals, and technology experts from across Europe and beyond to explore the future of digital archives and historical knowledge infrastructures.
